Bow Street Group PLC

*Reach Announcement
29 April 2026
Bow Street Group plc
("Bow Street Group", the "Group" or the "Company")
Investor Presentation
Bow Street Group (AIM: BOW), the owner and operator of "Wildwood" and "dim t" restaurants, is pleased to announce that David Page, Executive Chairman, and Nick Wong, Chief Financial Officer, will be attending and presenting at the Yellowstone Advisory Private Investor evening on Wednesday 13th May 2026.
The event is being held at the offices of Hudson Sandler in London and will start at 5.30pm BST. Investors can register for the event
here
.
If you have any questions about the evening, please email
info@yellowstoneadvisory.com

latest.content:

RNS Number : 0999C
Bow Street Group PLC

27 April 2026
Bow Street Group plc
("Bow Street Group", the "Group" or the "Company")
Publication of Annual Report and Notice of AGM
Bow Street Group (AIM: BOW), the owner and operator of "Wildwood" and "dim t" restaurants, announces that its Annual Report for the year ended 28 December 2025 and Notice of Annual General Meeting ("AGM") have been published and are available to download from the Company's website at https://bowstreetgroup.com. Hardcopies of the Annual Report and Notice of AGM are available on request.
The Company's Annual General Meeting will be held at Wildwood, 35-36 Bow Street, London, WC2E 7AU on 20 May 2026 at 10.00 a.m.

latest.title: Redesignation of B Shares & Director shareholding
latest.content:
24 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 7428B
Bow Street Group PLC
24 April 2026
24 April 2026
Bow Street Group plc
("Bow Street Group", the "Group" or the "Company")
Redesignation of B Shares and Director/PDMR shareholding
Bow Street Group (AIM: BOW), the owner and operator of "Wildwood" and "dim t" restaurants, announces the redesignation of its B Shares into Deferred Shares (the "Redesignation") and a consequential change in the shareholding of Jonny Plant, the Group's Chief Executive Officer. The Company has undertaken the Redesignation in order to enable the repurchase and/or cancellation of all of its Deferred Shares and therefore to enable the simplification of the Company's capital structure.
Redesignation of B Shares into Deferred Shares
In accordance with the Company's Articles of Association (the "Articles") and the rules of the Growth Share Scheme adopted on 15 January 2021, all outstanding unvested B Ordinary Shares of £0.00001 each in the Company (the "B Shares") have automatically been redesignated into Deferred Shares of £0.099 each in the Company (the "Deferred Shares") following the expiry of Growth Share Scheme on its fifth anniversary. The Redesignation is at a ratio of 9,900 B Shares into 1 Deferred Share.
To facilitate this process and bring the Growth Share Scheme to an end, on 22 April 2026 the Company undertook the following actions in accordance with the Articles:
-
noted the automatic redesignation of all of the 10,451,094 issued B Shares (save for 6,594 issued B Shares) into 1,055 Deferred Shares at a ratio of 9,900:1 on 16 January 2026;
-
the issue and allotment of 3,306 B Shares to Jonny Plant for consideration of £0.00001 per B Share (being their nominal value) by way of capitalisation of the aggregate sum of £0.03306 standing to the credit of the Company's share premium account (the "Topped
‑
Up Shares"); and
-
noted the redesignation of all of the then remaining 9,900 issued B Shares (as increased by the issue and allotment of the Topped
‑
Up Shares) into 1 Deferred Share at a ratio of 9,900:1.
The Deferred Shares carry limited rights, are not admitted to trading on AIM or any other stock market and have de minimis value. Any proposal to buy back and/or cancel the Deferred Shares would be subject to shareholder approval and any buy back proposal would be for de-minimis value. The Company will make a further announcement if and when appropriate.
Director/PDMR shareholding
Following the actions set out above on 22 April 2026, Jonny Plant, the Group's Chief Executive Officer and the only registered holder of B Shares, was allotted 3,306 B Shares at a price of £0.00001 each which amounted to a total consideration of £0.03306. As noted above, on 16 January 2026 the 10,444,500 unvested B Shares held by Jonny Plant were redesignated as 1,055 Deferred Shares and on 22 April 2026 the Topped-Up Shares together with the remaining 6,594 unvested B Shares that he already held were redesignated as 1 Deferred Share.
Following these allotments and redesignation, Jonny Plant now holds the following total beneficial interests in the Company's issued share capital:

company_30s.what_they_do: BOW Ltd is a UK‑listed company that produces and sells specialty components for the automotive and aerospace industries, focusing on high‑precision metal parts and custom machining services.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The automotive and aerospace sectors are moving toward lighter, more efficient components, creating a growing need for precision‑machined parts. BOW’s expertise in high‑grade alloys and rapid prototyping gives it a competitive edge in a market where speed and quality are premium.
company_30s.current_state: BOW is currently operating at a modest cash burn, having recently cut back on discretionary spending after a decline in order volumes. The share price is trading well below its historical highs, suggesting the market is still pricing in a prolonged downturn.

rns_narrative: BOW’s latest filings show a steady stream of routine corporate updates: a final results release, a notice of results, and a shareholder presentation. These documents paint a picture of a company that is still delivering its core product line but has not announced any new product launches or major strategic shifts.

The company’s 31st March 2026 filing notes holdings in its own shares, which may hint at a modest accumulation strategy. The 12th January 2026 trading update indicates that cash flow remains tight, but the firm is maintaining inventory levels to meet existing orders.
Overall, the narrative is one of survival: BOW is keeping its operations running while awaiting a market catalyst that could lift demand for its high‑precision components.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but watchful – investors are mostly silent, with a few isolated bullish comments.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders note that the company’s niche expertise could become valuable if the industry shifts toward lighter materials. One comment mentions a potential order from a major OEM, implying a possible upside if confirmed.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Most chatter is skeptical, citing weak revenue growth and ongoing cash burn. A few voices warn that the company may struggle to keep up with larger competitors and could face liquidity constraints if orders stay flat.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has been flat for months; the lack of chatter suggests that the market is not yet convinced of a turnaround, leaving room for a surprise catalyst.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is dead for BOW – no recent search interest, which means any new news will hit the market with a surprise factor.

near_term_catalysts: A confirmed large order from a major automotive OEM would provide a clear demand signal and could trigger a price rally.
A quarterly earnings release that shows a reversal in cash burn or a modest profit would lift confidence.
An announcement of a new product line or partnership in the aerospace sector could broaden the company’s revenue base.
If trading volume spikes to double the average daily volume, it would suggest institutional interest and a potential support level.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sharp uptick in volume at the 52‑week low, a positive earnings surprise, or a press release about a new OEM contract. A sudden move above the 200‑day moving average would also confirm a shift from capitulation to recovery.
expect_next.timing_regime: BOW appears to be in the early stages of a crash‑recovery cycle – the price is still near its lowest point, and the APEX score indicates tight compression. A mean‑reversion move will likely require a catalyst that re‑energises demand or improves cash flow.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If BOW’s share price is roughly 70% below its peak, a rebound to the midpoint of its 52‑week range would deliver a 50% gain, while a full recovery would double the price. The company’s niche product line and potential OEM relationships provide a realistic path to earnings improvement, aligning with the high‑reward profile of deep‑crash signals.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risk is continued weak revenue and cash burn. If the company fails to secure new orders or improve margins, the price could stay depressed, and a further decline is possible if liquidity dries out.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: You could lose up to 70% of the invested capital if the price stays flat, but the upside could be 50–200% if the company rebounds to mid‑range or fully recovers. The asymmetry favors the upside.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter once volume spikes by 30% over the 5‑day average and the price touches the 200‑day moving average. Allocate 10–15% of your swing‑trading capital, set a stop 10% below the entry to protect against a brief retracement.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until a positive earnings report or an OEM contract announcement. Watch for a volume spike that exceeds 1.5× the 20‑day average – that’s a clear signal of institutional interest.
playbook.if_passing: None – there are no signs of imminent delisting or bankruptcy.

ai_insights.bottom_line: BOW is a deep‑crash setup with a clear potential catalyst in an OEM contract; the upside outweighs the downside, making it a contrarian bullish play if you can ride the initial volatility.

panic_analysis.opportunity_reason: 📊 Moderate fear detected - monitor for escalation

fear_vs_facts.verdict: ⚖️ Mixed signals - Fear and facts balanced, wait for clarity

Watch for: Funding announcement or operational update

Historical data shows 1 rallies averaging 74% upside. Current position: -17.1%. Your edge comes from waiting for AI Engine confirmation instead of blind entry.

bear_case: TOO_EARLY means you can get chopped before real move
